# How to contribute

Great that you are willing to contribute to this project. Luckily for this project I don't have any strict rules in place for contributing. There are only a few things I ask:

## Did you find a bug?

- Ensure the bug has not already been reported before by searching the existing issues.
- Please create an issue with and label it as a bug. Write a clear description of exactly how you managed to trigger the bug as best you can. This helps me reproduce the bug and makes fixing a lot easier.

## Did you write a patch to fix an existing bug?

- Please open a new pull request with your fix.
- Include a detailed description with what you fixed and how you approached the problem.

## Did you write a new feature or expand existing functionality?

- You can open up a pull request with your feature.
- Include a detailed description with what you added to the project and how the feature works.
- Please do not open an issue on GitHub. Making a pull request allows me to review it directly and keep the issues tab clear.

## Contributing rules

- Please write clear understandable code that follows the code conventions of the project. You can take a look at the code base to get an idea for what is expected.
- Please write brief but clear commit messages. The commit message should be a reflection of what has been changed.
- Keep the files and folder structure organized.
